chapter: Chapter 4: From the Diary of Anne Frank

questions.question: What is the full name of the author of the diary?
questions.answer: Her full name is Anneliese Marie Frank.

questions.question: On which birthday did Anne receive her diary?
questions.answer: She received the diary on her thirteenth birthday.

questions.question: Why did Anne start writing a diary?
questions.answer: She started writing because she felt she didn't have a real friend to talk to.

questions.question: What name did Anne give to her diary?
questions.answer: She named it 'Kitty'.

questions.question: Which city did Anne's family move to when they left Germany?
questions.answer: They moved to Amsterdam in Holland.

questions.question: Who was the older sister of Anne Frank?
questions.answer: Her older sister was Margot Frank.

questions.question: What was the name of Anne’s father?
questions.answer: Her father's name was Otto Frank.

questions.question: What subject did Mr. Keesing teach?
questions.answer: Mr. Keesing taught Mathematics.

questions.question: Why did Mr. Keesing give Anne extra homework?
questions.answer: Because she was very talkative in his class.

questions.question: What was the title of the first essay Anne had to write?
questions.answer: The title was 'A Chatterbox'.

questions.question: How many essays did Mr. Keesing assign to Anne as punishment?
questions.answer: He assigned her a total of three essays.

questions.question: In what year did Anne's parents emigrate to Holland?
questions.answer: Her father emigrated in 1933, followed by her mother later that year.

questions.question: Where were Anne and Margot sent to live while their parents were in Holland?
questions.answer: They were sent to Aachen to stay with their grandmother.

questions.question: How does Anne describe herself as a student?
questions.answer: She was a good student but was often in trouble for talking.

questions.question: What did Anne say she inherited from her mother?
questions.answer: She said she inherited the habit of talking too much.

questions.question: Who was Anne's headmistress at the Montessori nursery school?
questions.answer: The headmistress was Mrs. Kuperus.

questions.question: What was the atmosphere in Anne’s class during the result days?
questions.answer: The students were nervous and 'quaking in their boots' about who would pass.

questions.question: What was the title of Anne's second essay punishment?
questions.answer: The title was 'An Incorrigible Chatterbox'.

questions.question: Who helped Anne write her third essay in poetry?
questions.answer: Her friend Sanne helped her.

questions.question: What was the third essay about?
questions.answer: It was a joke about a father swan who bit his three ducklings to death.

questions.question: How did Mr. Keesing react to the poem?
questions.answer: He took the joke well and read it to the class.

questions.question: Does Anne think paper is more patient than people?
questions.answer: Yes, she believes 'paper has more patience than people'.

questions.question: When did Anne’s grandmother die?
questions.answer: She died in January 1942.

questions.question: What did Anne do to remember her grandmother on her own birthday?
questions.answer: She lit a candle for her grandmother.

questions.question: What is the date of the first diary entry mentioned in the lesson?
questions.answer: The date is 20th June 1942.
